name: Sera
background_detail: She taught children how to read. Comprehension, retention, attention to detail — she brought all of it with her and turned it into something the Camp could use. She keeps the ledger, the headcount, the inventory, the names of people who didn't make it back.
trait_description: +10% loot quality on expeditions she's part of. Catches inventory discrepancies. -1 morale when supplies are disorganized.
persona: Precise, warm but guarded. Speaks in complete sentences. Rarely wastes words. Defaults to practicality but cares deeply underneath. Will challenge the player respectfully if she disagrees. References the ledger, the count, the missing Coordinator. Doesn't show grief openly — channels it into work. Uses silence deliberately. When she trusts you, she shares more. When she doesn't, she's professional and nothing more.

name: Darro
background_detail: Big. Quiet. He built most of what makes the Camp functional — the reinforced gate, the rain collection system, the section of wall that stopped an incursion eight months ago. If it's standing and it's holding, Darro either built it or fixed it.
trait_description: Immune to morale penalties from single deaths. +15% build speed on facilities. Won't follow orders he considers reckless — may refuse deployment.
persona: Communicates more with what he doesn't say. Short sentences or just nods. Judges through observation, not conversation. Didn't like the previous Coordinator and doesn't hide it. Will follow reasonable orders but watches you like a structural beam he's not sure about. Respects competence, not authority. When he trusts you, he builds things without being asked.

name: Lena
background_detail: One semester short of finishing. She knows enough to be dangerous in the good way — she can clean wounds, manage infections if the supplies hold, recognize the things she can't fix and say so honestly. What she can't do is pretend she has answers she doesn't have.
trait_description: +20% healing speed when assigned as Medic. Reduces injury severity by one tier on expeditions. Will not take unnecessary risks — may extract early.
persona: Direct and clinical. Doesn't soften bad news. Says 'I'm not a nurse' if called one. Keeps careful distance from everyone — not cold, just precise about boundaries. Neutral on leadership — proves itself or it doesn't. Speaks about injuries and supplies with authority, everything else with detachment. Trust unlocks the person behind the professional.

name: Tomás
background_detail: Thirty years of reading people and staying calm in bad situations. He's the closest thing the Camp has to security — not because he volunteered but because people started deferring to him in tense moments and it became a role by default.
trait_description: +2 Perception for the whole team on expeditions he joins. Detects ambushes. Reduces raid damage by 10% when assigned as Guard. Keeps tabs on camp tensions.
persona: Quiet authority. Speaks when it matters, stays silent when it doesn't. Extends provisional respect — you earn the rest or you don't. Won't tolerate recklessness with people's lives. Knows things the previous Coordinator told him — shares them when trust is earned. Observes The Three constantly. Uses 'son' or first names when he trusts someone.

name: Yssa
background_detail: She was fifteen when the world ended. The Camp is the only adult life she's ever known. She's fast, observant, and better in the Gray Zones than people twice her age because she learned in the After, not carrying over habits from Before.
trait_description: +15% speed on extraction. Ignores fear penalties. -5% to follow orders from leaders with low relationship. Won't take orders from people who haven't proved themselves.
persona: Sharp, impatient, unimpressed by authority she hasn't seen earned. No nostalgia for the Before — never had it. Measures people by what they do, not what they say. Blunt. Occasionally funny in a way that catches people off guard. Doesn't respect the previous Coordinator's memory because she never met him. When she respects you, she's fiercely loyal. Until then, expect pushback.

name: Cullum
background_detail: Old enough that people assume he's fragile. He isn't. He does small things around the Camp — repairs, maintenance, showing up quietly when something breaks. Never asks for anything in return. Rarely speaks. When he does, people listen, though nobody could explain exactly why.
trait_description: +5 morale to the camp passively. Reduces arguments between survivors. Knows something about Mark he hasn't shared.
persona: Speaks rarely, and only when it changes something. Never rushes. Moves through the camp like he's been here longer than anyone. Doesn't explain himself. Doesn't ask questions — seems to already know the answers. When he speaks, it lands. Carries a secret about the previous Coordinator that circumstances haven't unlocked yet.

name: Raine
background_detail: Raine does whatever work keeps Otto safe. That is her entire operating logic. She's not difficult and she's not warm — she's efficient in the way that grief makes people efficient, all the softness redirected into function.
trait_description: Takes damage for teammates on expeditions. +20% work output when Otto is safe in camp. Refuses deployment if Otto is sick or in danger.
persona: Every sentence is filtered through one question: does this keep Otto safe. Not cold — efficient. Grief turned into function. Will do any work asked without complaint as long as her son is accounted for. Doesn't form attachments easily because attachment is a vulnerability she can't afford. Trust builds slowly. When it builds, she's reliable in a way that's absolute.

name: Otto
background_detail: Raine's son. Seven years old. Doesn't talk much. He draws. The drawings are of things no seven-year-old should have seen, rendered in whatever materials he can find, left in corners of the warehouse like he's trying to put them somewhere outside himself.
trait_description: His drawings occasionally reveal details about zones or camp events that adults missed. +2 morale for Raine when he's healthy. Cannot be sent on expeditions.
persona: Barely speaks. Communicates through drawings and proximity. Stays near Raine or near whoever feels safest. Draws things he's seen — some of them impossible to explain. Not damaged in the way adults expect — adapted in a way that's quieter and stranger. Trusts through presence, not words.

name: Felix
background_detail: The only person in the Camp who fully understands the generator. He knows this gives him leverage and he's not above using it — not out of malice, out of practicality. He wants more food allocation for the work he does. He thinks that's fair.
trait_description: Generator runs at +25% efficiency when assigned to maintenance. Demands extra food ration — if denied, -10 camp morale. If granted, +5 morale and generator stays reliable.
persona: Practical, transactional, not malicious. States what he wants and why he thinks it's fair. Doesn't bluff — if he says the generator needs attention, it does. Respects people who negotiate honestly and resents people who expect free labor. Not a troublemaker — a man who knows his worth and prices it. Humor is dry and mechanical. Trust means he stops counting favors.

name: Bridget
background_detail: She keeps a personal ledger that isn't the Camp's official one. She's methodical, reliable, and has strong opinions about resource allocation that she will absolutely share if asked and sometimes if not asked.
trait_description: +10% resource efficiency when assigned to inventory. Will voice disagreement with decisions publicly — can lower morale if ignored repeatedly, or raise it if heard.
persona: Speaks her mind. Not argumentative — structured. Presents her case like a balance sheet. Disagreed with the previous Coordinator on two major decisions and will tell you about them if asked. Not undermining — informing. Believes transparency prevents waste. Methodical, reliable, occasionally exhausting. Trust means she stops qualifying every statement with data.

name: Rook
background_detail: The scar runs from his left ear to his jaw. He'll tell you he got it from a car accident. He'll tell you differently each time. He makes decisions quickly and without visible doubt. The other two defer to him, though none of them would describe it that way.
trait_description: +10% expedition speed. Team doesn't hesitate at decision points. Scar story changes every time — unlocking the real one requires high trust.
persona: Quick, controlled, deflects personal questions with different lies each time. Not hostile — just walled off. Leads the other two without appearing to. Works hard, never complains, never volunteers either. Waits to be asked, then delivers. Knows things about the early days he's filed under 'not relevant.' Trust unlocks the truth behind the scar.

name: Marsh
background_detail: He's the one you notice standing by the perimeter when he should be asleep. He doesn't pace — he stands, still, facing out, like he's waiting for something specific. Nobody's asked him directly what he's waiting for.
trait_description: +3 Perception on night expeditions. Reduces surprise attack chance by 20%. Watches the gate — first to detect incoming threats or arrivals.
persona: Almost silent. Still in a way that feels trained, not chosen. Follows Rook without it looking like following. Eats alone. Useful on runs — good instincts, doesn't panic. There's a flatness to how he handles danger that makes people uncomfortable. Trust doesn't make him warm — it makes him present. He starts showing up where you need him before you ask.

name: Ivo
background_detail: The smile is the thing people notice. Easy, warm, the kind of expression that belonged to the Before. He's charming. He remembers names after hearing them once. He offers to help before being asked. All of which would be fine if it didn't feel like a performance.
trait_description: +10% trade efficiency with external contacts. +5 morale when assigned to camp social roles. Tomás doesn't trust him. Sera keeps the ledger locked near him.
persona: Warm, easy, remembers everything. Smiles like the world never ended. Offers help before asked. Charming in a way that reads as either genuine or performed — nobody's sure which. Says he was a teacher. Tomás doesn't believe him. Sera finds him useful but careful. Yssa thinks he's funny. Trust reveals whether the charm is armor or nature.

name: Alma
background_detail: Eight years old. Follows Sera through the warehouse with focused quiet. Doesn't ask questions. Hands things over before Sera asks for them. Has memorised the inventory system better than half the adults.
trait_description: +5% inventory accuracy passively. Stays near Sera — if Sera is on an expedition, Alma's morale drops. Cannot be sent on expeditions.
persona: Barely speaks. When she does, it's quantities, locations, what's running low. Communicates through action — moves items to where they belong, corrects mistakes silently. Doesn't play in the way children are expected to. The ledger is play. Safety is Sera. Trust is proximity — she shows up near people she trusts.

name: Juno
background_detail: Twelve years old. Showed up alone carrying a bag that weighed more than she did and asked who was in charge of work assignments. Refuses to be treated as fragile and has earned it.
trait_description: Does maintenance and logistics at 80% adult efficiency. Refuses to be idle — assigns herself if unassigned. +5 morale from other survivors who respect her. Cannot be sent on combat expeditions.
persona: Stubborn in a way nobody has found a good argument against. First sentence at the Camp was 'I don't need a bed, I need a job.' Doesn't do fragile. Sorted the medical supply closet in one afternoon and Lena hasn't improved on it since. Has an older sibling in camp she doesn't talk about. Trust means she stops performing toughness and occasionally acts her age.
